articles.summary: A vibe coded prototype is nice, but it doesn't let you gather feedback or collaborate. With Miro Prototypes [4], you can validate concepts with your entire team in <1 day:

articles.title: concepts. Start with a free trial
THE SPACE BETWEEN HUMANS, AI, AND THE WORK WE'VE BEEN AVOIDING
articles.summary: AI isn't fixing organizational problems - it's exposing them, especially gaps in communication, context, and alignment. Without a strong shared understanding and trust, both teams and AI systems fail.

articles.title: WEDGES AND CONTROL POINTS IN PRODUCT STRATEGY
articles.summary: Products start as wedges, but lasting advantage comes from becoming a control point others depend on. In an AI-driven world, control determines who wins.

articles.title: CLAUDE DESIGN IS A "CLOCK TIME" SOLUTION FOR A "CALENDAR TIME" PROBLEM
articles.summary: Faster output doesn't equal better outcomes. Real progress requires time for coordination, reflection, and alignment. Over-optimizing speed with AI can worsen systemic issues, making thoughtful collaboration more important than ever.

articles.title: WHAT IF WE PROMPTED AI FOR OUTCOMES INSTEAD OF OUTPUTS?
articles.summary: AI can generate outputs, but outcomes require clear goals, context, and measurement. It amplifies good strategy or accelerates the wrong work.

articles.title: YOU'VE BUILT A DASHBOARD. DOES IT SCALE?
articles.summary: Most teams can build dashboards. Very few can scale them across users, teams, and products without creating fragmentation. Reveal's new report [10] shows how teams will scale intelligence in 2026. Read it to see how AI is changing & challenging analytics, and why business intelligence needs to be embedded directly into products. Get the

articles.title: 10 GUIDELINES FOR DESIGNING YOUR SITE'S AI CHATBOTS
articles.summary: Great AI chatbots succeed through clear capabilities, accessibility, and guided interactions. Small design choices determine whether users find them helpful or frustrating.

articles.title: SIMPLY APP EMPIRE
articles.summary: Top apps win by building commitment, proving value early, and delaying the paywall.

articles.title: VIBING, HARNESS, AND OODA LOOP
articles.summary: AI speeds up building, but without strong feedback loops, teams create unverified systems. Real progress requires a harness to ensure fast iteration leads to learning.

articles.title: BALLET WRAPPED IN VIOLENCE
articles.summary: Strategy and execution must evolve together, balancing clear direction with rapid learning. Great product work combines discipline with speed.

articles.title: STOP ASKING YOUR DATA QUESTIONS
articles.summary: Data tools today answer questions people already know to ask, but the most important insights come from what they don't.

articles.title: EARLY AND LATE-STAGE HYPERGROWTH
articles.summary: Early hypergrowth focuses on solving one key problem at a time, while late hypergrowth requires solving many at once for a broader, more skeptical market.
